findings.title: `specflow-analyze` migration leaves `openspec/specs` ownership undefined
findings.detail: The proposal says `specflow-analyze.ts` will move both `openspec/specs` and `openspec/changes` directory traversal behind store APIs, but it only defines a new `ChangeArtifactStore.listChanges()` method. That covers cross-change enumeration only; it does not define which interface owns baseline spec listing under `openspec/specs` or what API shape design should use there. Add the owning interface and required operations for both change enumeration and spec enumeration, or explicitly remove `openspec/specs` from this refactor’s scope.

findings.title: `specflow-prepare-change` depends on change-scaffold semantics the proposal does not specify
findings.detail: The proposal says `specflow-prepare-change.ts` should stop constructing `changeDir` directly and go through `ChangeArtifactStore`, but that command must distinguish between 'change scaffold already exists' and 'new change must be created' even when `proposal.md` is still missing. A file-level `read`/`write`/`exists` abstraction is not sufficient to design this safely. Specify the store/API behavior for detecting an existing scaffolded change and the expected flow when only the change container exists.

findings.title: Stated outcome is broader than the listed implementation scope
findings.detail: The Why section says this work completes the abstraction so core no longer knows the artifact path layout, but the proposed changes only cover five bin files. That leaves a mismatch between the claimed outcome and the actual scope, which is likely to create spec drift during design. Either narrow the objective to 'remove direct path construction from the listed bins' or expand the scope/acceptance criteria to include any remaining non-adapter path-layout logic that must also move behind the store.
